Good game, good concept, but so pay to win. My first ever lobby I join I’m in the lobby with the number one player, top of the leaderboard. The match starts, he turns big and starts punching everyone off and the regular players can’t do anything about it. Good games like this get ruined by pay to win aspects. Please stop this
Floor is lava is amazing. I would install it.

Just played this game, and its really fun! I really only have a few problems. Like for example the size changer, its incredibly unfair for a lot of players. Like for example, in fireball they change to be small because its harder to hit them, in tag they become big when tagged to easily tag others, and small when they arent it to be harder to tag. I think it should be removed or balanced, like maybe for certain games they can only a bit smaller or bit bigger, not to big, not to small. It also takes quite a while to play a game, i think the waiting times are too long, like the players vote, and it takes up to 5 minutes or more to actually get into the game. I love the idea of punching, i think it should be kept as is, and love the idea for blocking punches! The game overall is fantastic but really only kept from a five star because the two issues i mentioned. I do also think that maybe a few power ups should be able to be bought with bananas, like for example buying the size changer for maybe 1000 bananas, however it doesnt give the same affect as purchasing it with money, so you can only become a certain ammount bigger and smaller, just to keep it fair for those who spent money on it.  Some functional cosmetics would be really cool, like maybe for example, might be a stretch, but maybe having like some kind of "lasso" type cosmetic, that you can point at someone to bring them over to you, with a cool down of course. I think that would be really fun!
This game is the best gorilla physic game out there, only complaint is the pay to win not only being a part of private lobbies.

I just got the game and I fell in love with it. It is so amazing. (Think I will be spending some money in it another time) I absolutely love the horror game mode it is so much fun. It’s so thrilling to be chased and really fun when others are. I love the glass bridge as well. All the other game modes are great too but the ones I mentioned are my favorites. Other than that I love the game keep up the good work and do a few updates every once in a while to keep people as excited as I am about the game
I played this game for the first time and I was amazed at how many game modes.
All I will say is pay to win ropes barley work and quality is bad I do not like, needs bug fixes.

reviews.review_title: Honest Review of “The Floor is Lava”
reviews.review_description:
This game is very fun to play, I love the quality of the maps and mechanics. 
  I found about the game, though, is that you have purchase the power-ups. It would be better if you added a one time power up for 500-1000 bananas, and instead of just buying them with real cash, you could buy with the ingame currency too. 
  I do like the smooth gameplay, the leaderboard system works well, and the cosmetics are decently priced. 
  Another con I noticed was that there’s not much instruction on where to go as the lava rises on the volcano map, maybe add some arrows where the player spawns. Also the settings for graphics are a bit tricky to navigate and could use more instruction. 
  Overall this game is great, and is obviously still a work in progress, excited for the new modes and maps soon.
Its a Great game you can play The FLOOR IS LAVA, HORROR, TAG so grab a friend and go climb Up...🗣

I’m not gonna be biased even though turkey is my friend. In my opinion the game is actually pretty good and developed pretty well. It also has so much effort put into it. It’s actually obvious he put a lot of effort since he did take like 4+ months to make this game. And turkey if ur reading this dm me hi bc I’m bored

I suppose Turkey is currently working in fixing stuff, but currently, the intro for the game is simple but interesting, and the lobby looks good to say the least, it has great ambience, and sfx, with great particles too. Considering Turkey only has been using the Unity Game Engine for around a month, I'm impressed, but unfortunately, there's nothing to do in the game other than wandering around the lobby, but I believe soon the main-game mechanic will be added to the game, yeah, that's another thing, the whole point of the game is the Rising Lava game, and it's not in the game, like, what?? And considering he's one of the best scripters I've met, does not help his case. But again, I believe that Turkey will soon add all the features making this a great and fun game.
